qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Remote code execution via skills code execution feature allows arbitrary code execution with no privilege validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes authentication bypass through password hash exposure and pass-the-hash attacks. This finding describes a remote code execution vulnerability in the skills code execution feature (SkillsInjectionHook). The root cause is missing privilege validation in the skills execution path, which is completely different from the advisory's root causes: weak SHA-256 hashing, hash exposure via /user/info, /user/update, /spend/users endpoints, and pass-the-hash acceptance at /v2/login. The vulnerable code paths, security mechanisms bypassed, and impact mechanisms are all distinct.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying vulnerability finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory GHSA-69x8-hrgq-fjj8 describes a three-part authentication bypass chain: (1) weak unsalted SHA-256 password hashing, (2) password hash exposure through /user/info, /user/update, and /spend/users API endpoints, and (3) pass-the-hash authentication at /v2/login accepting raw SHA-256 hashes as passwords. The qualifying finding describes a code injection RCE vulnerability in the skills code execution feature with a different root cause (missing privilege validation), different vulnerable endpoints, and a different vulnerability class. The finding does not identify the vulnerable authentication endpoints, password hash exposure, or pass-the-hash login bypass described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory summary: 'Password hash exposure and pass-the-hash authentication bypass' affecting user authentication system
Advisory details: Three-part chain - weak SHA-256 hashing, hash exposure via /user/info, /user/update, /spend/users, pass-the-hash at /v2/login
Skills_rce_injection finding: RCE in skills code execution - different root cause (missing privilege validation), different vulnerable feature, different vulnerability class
Finding does not mention password hashing, password hash exposure, or authentication bypass via pass-the-hash
